mission.title: harbor: docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header
mission.description: docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header. This issue affects 23+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
should pull proxy cache repo images success.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor to include the manifest descriptor fix
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the ManifestExist fix:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```
After upgrading, Docker 20.10+ clients should be able to pull proxy cache images without errors.

steps.title: Confirm docker 20.10 pull proxy cache image failed: Error… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The issue was caused by Harbor's ManifestExist method returning only a digest string instead of a full Descriptor. Docker 20.10 requires the full manifest descriptor (including mediaType and size) for proxy cache pulls. The fix updates ManifestExist to return a Descriptor instead of just a digest, allowing Docker 20.10 clients to correctly pull images through Harbor's proxy cache.
codeSnippets:
# docker.io is a proxy cache project
docker pull ${HARBOR_HOST}/docker.io/library/alpine:3.12
Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header

docker --version
Docker version 18.06.3-ce, build d7080c1

docker pull my-harbor-domain/dockerhub/library/alpine:3.12
Error response from daemon: missing or empty Content-Type header

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
